steps: Dissect larval fillets in 0 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 solution at room temperature.
To trigger CME, stimulate samples with 90 mM K + /0.5 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 solution for 1 min or 60 mM K + /1 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 solution for 10 min. 00:10:00
To induce ADBE, subject larval fillets to stimulation of a 90 mM K + /2 mM or 5 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 solution in the presence or lack of 10 mM La 3+ for 10 min. 00:10:00
Rinse the samples with 0 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 solution. (1/3)
Rinse the samples with 0 mM Ca 2+ HL-3 solution. (3/3)
Fix further at least for 12 h in 4% paraformaldehyde/1% glutaraldehyde/0.1 M cacodylic acid (pH 7.2) solution 12:00:00
Rinse the samples with 0.1 M cacodylic acid (pH 7.2) solution.
Fix the samples in 1% OsO4/0.1 M cacodylic acid solution at room temperature for 3 h. 03:00:00
Subject the samples to a series of dehydration from 30 to 100% ethanol.
After 100% ethanol dehydration, incubate the samples in propylene, mixture of propylene and resin and pure resin.
Capture the images of type Ib boutons using Tecnai G2 Spirit TWIN (FEI Company) and Gatan CCD Camera (794.10.BP2MultiScanTM) at ³ 4,400 x magnifications.
Count the size of SVs and bulk cisternae and the area of type Ib boutons using Image J.

Identify type Ib boutons by multiple layers of subsynaptic reticulum. The radius of bulk cisternae is calculated from A(area)=πr 2 . Isolated membranous structures larger than 80 nm in diameter were defined as bulk cisternae.
creator: Chi-Kuang Yao
